summary: Soundboard capture of a legendary June‑13‑87 Ventura gig – stellar second set with Masterpiece, Shakedown and a soaring Morning Dew, recorded clean enough to enjoy despite occasional thin mix on the keyboards.
review: Fans rave about this show as one of the best of ’87, highlighting the surprise debut of “Masterpiece”, a punchy “Shakedown” opener for the second set and Jerry’s transcendent jam on “Morning Dew”. The recording is generally clear (soundboard) though a few listeners note a thin synth mix that slightly colors the early‑set sound.

ai_rating.rationale: Average star rating of 4.3 across ten reviews, with overwhelmingly positive comments about performance and only modest technical complaints, yielding a strong overall score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Overall clear and listenable; some reviewers describe the early‑set mix as thin or Brent’s synths overpowering, but no major dropouts or distortion reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Generous, meaty ’87 set with a mellow first half and an energetic second set that never let up; the surprise debut of “Masterpiece” and a powerful “Morning Dew” jam make it a fan favorite.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for vocal intensity (“singing right at me”) and emotive guitar work, especially on “Morning Dew” and the fast “Masterpiece”.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s synths noted – occasionally thin or over‑bearing, but also providing tasteful texture (e.g., “Push”, “Estimated”).
